description: Uniform active-surface routing (ADR 01081) for the PROCESS kind: a process opened via startSurface becomes the active surface, so a `type` step that omits `surface` sends its keystrokes to that process's stdin. The routing proof is two-fold: the surface-less type PASSes in a context with no browser at all (pre-ADR it fell through to the browser path), and a later explicit type's `waitUntil.stdio` matches the REPL output the SURFACE-LESS keystrokes produced (the stdio matcher checks buffered history, so the match can only come from the earlier write). Also proves a process registered by runShell `background` does NOT hijack the active surface — only startSurface and explicit references switch it. Node-only, so it runs and PASSes on every platform; unique names/ports keep it safe under concurrentRunners.

tests.description: Start node's REPL via the process descriptor, type arithmetic WITHOUT a surface (routes to the active process), then assert the result landed via an explicit reference whose stdio condition matches the output the surface-less write produced. A final surface-less type proves the explicit reference kept the process active.

A background process registered by runShell (not startSurface) must NOT become the active surface: after starting one, a surface-less type still routes to the startSurface-opened REPL — proven by an explicit stdio match on the REPL's output, which only exists if the surface-less keystrokes went there.
